summary: TEST A (ADD): PASS locally on parent — AddX1784676213 (dob 2012-05-15 Male, age 14) added and survives parent reload (kids=[amu, john, AddX...]). TEST B (DELETE just-added child): PASS locally on parent — Remove + Save + Reload leaves [amu, john], AddX gone. TEST C (DELETE pre-existing 'john'): FAIL — Save applies locally (kids=[amu], ts=1784676320726) but every one of 4 consecutive reloads over ~30s restores kids=[amu, john] with the SAME childrenUpdatedAt=1784676320726 (i.e. the timestamp survives but registeredChildren gets reverted). Manager side: Manager Dashboard shows Total Members=2 with parent's dlbc_players row still containing [amu, john] plus roster children rows for both amu AND john — 'john' NEVER cleared. Regression PASS: parent Notifications shows only real items (Membership payment due + 'Welcome to the new season!' 1 Sept 2025); no banned placeholder strings (Neptune BC / fundraising event 25 February / monthly membership €50 / Tuesday training moved). Console during TEST C save: '[app_state] member contribution sync failed TypeError: Failed to fetch' — the Supabase contribution push is still failing intermittently.

ui_bugs.issue: CRITICAL: this is the residual root cause of the john-resurrection regression. mergePlayersForSync still uses 'prefer LONGER registeredChildren list' (lines 823-826) and does NOT consult childrenUpdatedAt. On parent reload, pullRemoteAppState fetches the shared dlbc_players blob (which the manager last flushed with [amu,john]), applySyncedPlayersValue calls mergePlayersForSync(local=[amu], remote=[amu,john]) and the merge picks the longer remote list → john is resurrected. The LWW timestamp fix was applied to mergeContributionIntoPlayers but NOT to mergePlayersForSync. The bug reproduces even though the local player row's childrenUpdatedAt=1784676320726 is strictly newer than anything on remote — the merge simply doesn't read that field for the shared blob path.
ui_bugs.selector: n/a (state sync layer, triggered on every page load / pullRemoteAppState)

ui_bugs.issue: HIGH: the 4x retry with 600ms backoff still ultimately fails from the preview browser ('TypeError: Failed to fetch' logged during TEST C). Even after all 4 attempts the contribution never lands, so remote/manager never see the delete. Because updateAuthUserData (PlayerDashboard:216) also uses supabase.auth.updateUser which can hit the same network issue, auth.user_metadata may also carry the stale [amu,john] set — although syncRegisteredChildrenFromAuthMetadata now correctly guards on authTs>localTs, if both auth AND app_state pushes fail (as observed), remote stays stale and mergePlayersForSync still wins over local.

integration_issues.flow: Parent Profile > Registered children > Remove pre-existing child 'john' > Save children  → parent reload / manager Members
integration_issues.issue: TEST C fails end-to-end. Parent: 4 reloads over ~30s and john resurrects every time (kids=[amu,john], ts=1784676320726 unchanged). Manager: Dashboard shows Total Members=2, and parent.registeredChildren in manager localStorage still holds [amu,john] with both roster child rows present.

action_items: P0 — Fix mergePlayersForSync to be last-write-wins on childrenUpdatedAt for a member's own row, mirroring the fix already applied to mergeContributionIntoPlayers. Concretely: in mergePlayersForSync/applySyncedPlayersValue, when merging a non-parentPlayerId row, if BOTH sides have childrenUpdatedAt set, prefer the side with the larger ts (taking its registeredChildren/childName/childDob verbatim) instead of preferring the longer list. If only one side has ts, prefer that side. This closes the reload-resurrection path even when the contribution push fails.

P0 — Also make applySyncedPlayersValue drop child-roster rows (parentPlayerId===owner.id) whose corresponding registeredChild is no longer present in the winning owner row (same 'honour deletions' logic already in mergeContributionIntoPlayers).

P1 — Investigate why supabase.from('app_state').upsert is failing with 'TypeError: Failed to fetch' from the preview browser even after 4x retries (all 4 attempts fail in one save). Likely candidates: intermittent egress DNS, RLS policy denying the upsert (surfacing as thrown error rather than {error}), or CORS preflight for the preview host. Add telemetry that distinguishes 'network Failed to fetch' vs '{error} row-level-security-denied' so we can act on it. Consider also flushing the fresh dlbc_players blob to the manager-shared key from the member client (guarded by RLS to only allow updating own row) as a redundant path.
P2 — Once P0 lands: re-run TEST C from a clean baseline (parent.registeredChildren=[amu,john]). Expected: reload keeps [amu]; manager Members shrinks to 1 within ~15s.

P3 — src/pages/PlayerDashboard.tsx (2219 lines) and src/lib/clubData.ts (2527 lines) still greatly exceed the 700-line guideline. Splitting ProfileTab + the sync layer would make these reload/race conditions much easier to reason about and to unit test.
critical_code_review_comments: The childrenUpdatedAt LWW fix is correct in spirit but was only applied to ONE of the two merge paths. mergeContributionIntoPlayers (lines 940-990) now correctly checks contribTs vs localTs AND honours deletions by trimming child rows not in the contribution. mergePlayersForSync (lines 806-838) — used by applySyncedPlayersValue for the shared dlbc_players blob — still uses the old 'largest set wins' heuristic (lines 823-826) that resurrects the deleted child. Because the manager periodically re-publishes the aggregated dlbc_players blob, any client on reload merges against it and loses the delete.

The 'auth.updateUser retry' in PlayerDashboard.updateAuthUserData is a good defensive measure but it does not close the loop when BOTH pushes fail — in TEST C we saw only the contribution warning, but the same network condition would affect auth.updateUser too. Consider persisting an outbound queue in localStorage (dlbc_pending_pushes) that a service worker or the next page load can drain, so a delete cannot be lost even if the tab closes before Supabase becomes reachable.

syncRegisteredChildrenFromAuthMetadata (lines 1000-1029) is now correct: it only restores children when authTs > localTs OR local was never edited. Verified during TEST C that local ts=1784676320726 remained intact across reloads, so this function did not overwrite. The revival must be coming from applySyncedPlayersValue → mergePlayersForSync.

Notifications logic remains clean: only real items ('Membership payment due for July 2026' + 'Welcome to the new season!' 1 Sept 2025). None of the banned placeholder strings (Neptune BC / fundraising event on 25 February / monthly membership payment of €50 / Tuesday training moved) appear. Read/Delete controls render correctly. Manager Dashboard 'Recent activity' still legitimately references 'Match scheduled: vs Neptune BC' from a real seeded match row (not a notification).

success_rate.frontend: 60% — TEST A pass (parent local), TEST B pass (parent local + manager implicit), TEST C FAIL on both parent and manager, notifications regression pass

test_credentials: Parent: cooler74.ea@gmail.com / test12 — post-run baseline is UNCHANGED at [amu, john] because TEST C's delete did not persist. Manager: manager@dublinlions.ie / lions2025.
seed_data_creation: Created child 'AddX1784676213' (dob 2012-05-15, Male) during TEST A. Deleted successfully during TEST B — parent roster reverted cleanly to [amu, john] after reload. Session artefact 'AddX1784676213' is no longer present in dlbc_players.
context_for_next_testing_agent: TEST A + TEST B are now solid. TEST C (delete pre-existing 'john') is STILL BROKEN on reload — john resurrects after each of 4 reloads with ts unchanged, so the resurrection is NOT via syncRegisteredChildrenFromAuthMetadata (which now guards on ts) but via mergePlayersForSync in applySyncedPlayersValue (path for the shared dlbc_players blob). The fix needs to be replicated there: LWW on childrenUpdatedAt + honour deletions of child roster rows. Also observed intermittent 'TypeError: Failed to fetch' from supabase.from('app_state').upsert even after 4x retry — add richer telemetry and consider a persistent outbound queue. Once mergePlayersForSync is patched, re-run TEST C from a fresh baseline (currently [amu, john] with parent.childrenUpdatedAt=1784676320726). Expected on parent: reload => [amu]. Expected on manager: Total Members shrinks to 1 within ~15s.
rca_of_the_issue: REPRODUCTION (TEST C, confirmed): 1) Parent baseline dlbc_players[parent].registeredChildren = [amu, john] with childrenUpdatedAt=1784676237530. 2) Open Manage children, click Remove on john card (verified DOM: text inputs after remove = [test(parent name), amu]). 3) Click Save children. 4) Immediately: dlbc_players[parent].registeredChildren = [amu], childrenUpdatedAt=1784676320726 — delete applied locally. Console warns '[app_state] member contribution sync failed TypeError: Failed to fetch' — Supabase push failed even after 4x retry. 5) page.reload() ×4 (over ~30s). 6) On every reload: dlbc_players[parent].registeredChildren = [amu, john], childrenUpdatedAt=1784676320726 (SAME as post-save). ROOT CAUSE: On reload, pullRemoteAppState fetches the shared dlbc_players blob (manager's aggregated snapshot with john still present because the contribution push never landed). applySyncedPlayersValue calls mergePlayersForSync(local, remote). mergePlayersForSync (src/lib/clubData.ts:806) uses playerRecordScore to pick primary, then at lines 823-826 sets registeredChildren = whichever side has the LONGER list. Since remote's parent row still has [amu, john] (length 2) and local has [amu] (length 1), the longer remote list wins → john resurrected. The childrenUpdatedAt field on local (1784676320726) is copied through as-is because it's part of the primary row spread, but the registeredChildren field is overridden by the length heuristic — this is why ts survives but children revert. The recent LWW timestamp fix was only added to mergeContributionIntoPlayers (contribution row path), NOT to mergePlayersForSync (shared blob path). MITIGATION (P0): Change mergePlayersForSync to prefer the side with the LARGER childrenUpdatedAt for a member's own registeredChildren (falling back to the current length heuristic only when neither side has a timestamp), AND replicate the 'drop child-roster rows omitted by the winning owner' logic from mergeContributionIntoPlayers into applySyncedPlayersValue so the manager's aggregated child rows are also trimmed when a member deletes a child. (P1) Add a persistent outbound queue in localStorage to survive Failed-to-fetch push loss across reloads.
